Shrine buildings
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}{{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}} Kawaraya 86[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Ōnamochi Shrine] (Nara Prefecture Shrine Office). |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 38.42 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 52|経度秒 = 8.33 |ISO = JP-29 |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamochi Mitama-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suseribime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}} |神体 ={{jalink|Imoseyama|Q112620631}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-grand}})
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{jalink|October 17|Q2962}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}} |本殿 = {{jalink|Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名 = Ōnana-miya |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Imoyama (Yoshino), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View of Imoyama from the opposite bank}}{{small|Torii of Ōnamochi Shrine at mid-left. Foreground: [[Yoshino River (Nara)|Yoshino River]].}}]] '''Ōnamochi Shrine''' (おおなもちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Kawaraya, {{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}, {{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and its former {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534|shrine rank}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. Its common name is also “Ōnana-miya”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. It stands at the foot of isolated {{jalink|Imoyama (Nara)|Q112339073|Imoyama}}, near the branching point of the old {{jalink|Ise Kaidō}} and old Higashi-Kumano Kaidō{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following threeExplanation board in precincts.. * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamochi Mitama-no-Kami}} * {{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suseribime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}} In documents, the '{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}' mentions “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami,” the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' '{{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' lists “Ōnamochi Shrine,” and in the Engishiki section on temporary festivals (Myojin festivals) appears “Ōnamochi Mitama Shrine, one seat,” indicating that from ancient times the principal deity was one seat of Ōnamuchi (another name of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944}}). How Sukunabikona came to be enshrined is unclear; theories include a later enshrinement based on a poem in volume 7 of the '{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' (quoted below), or that he was a deity of Seyama on the opposite bank{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. The 'Jinja Meisaichō' lists four seats: Ōnamochi-no-Kami, Ōnamochi Mitama-no-Kami, consort deity Suseribime-no-Kami, and Sukunabikona-no-Kami{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. The shrine stands at the foot of {{jalink|Imoyama (Nara)|Q112339073|Imoyama}} (elevation 249 m[http://www.town.yoshino.nara.jp/kanko-event/meisho-kanko/shisetsu/ryumon-nakaryumon/imoyamajuso.html Imoyama forest] (Yoshino Town website).). Since Imoyama is also called “Imiyama (taboo mountain)” and a taboo tradition of not cutting trees remains, it is inferred that primitive {{jalink|animistic|Q43004}} worship of Imoyama as a {{jalink|shintai mountain|Q1595289}} was the shrine’s origin{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), the {{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025|divine rank}} of “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami” in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} was raised from Junior First Rank to Senior First Rank{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. At that time, in Yamato Province only {{jalink|Kasuga Taisha|Q714559}} had reached this highest rank; this promotion was extremely early even nationally, though the reason is not fully clear{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. One theory is that reverence rose because it stood at a key transport point connecting the capital in the {{jalink|Nara Basin|Q10942269}} to Yoshino{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' '{{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in {{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}, Yamato Province, as “Ōnamochi Shrine, Myojin-grand, Tsukinami/Ainame/Niiname,” and was ranked a {{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}} while being granted offerings at court’s {{jalink|Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161}}, {{jalink|Ainame Festival|Q11581944}}, and {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. In Engishiki temporary festivals, in the Myojin-festival section among 285 seats appears “Ōnamochi Mitama Shrine, one seat”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, it appears in 'Ōmiwa Bunshin Ruisha-shō narabi ni Fubi' as “Imose Shrine”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}}, 'Yamato-shi' records a {{jalink|jingū-ji|Q1074971}} called Daikai-ji in the precincts (site of current shrine office) and Shiobiu-chi in front of the shrine{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. Shiobiu-chi was said to spring salt water, and purification was once performed there{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In early {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, Daikai-ji was abolished; honji-buddhas and ritual implements were moved to Bukoku-ji, and the Daihannya-kyō to Unsen-ji{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), Jan. 27: from Junior First Rank to Senior First Rank ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') - written as “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami.” === Precincts === File:Onamochi-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Onamochi-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Onamochi-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office
{{small|Formerly site of Daikai-ji, a {{jalink|jingū-ji|Q1074971}}.}} File:Onamochi-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Sessha and massha === [[File:Onamochi-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Precinct shrine}}]] The following four precinct shrines are present. * Wakamiya Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. * Suijin Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}. * Konpira Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856}}. * Inari Shrine - deity: Inasō no Kami. === Cultural properties === === National Natural Monument === * Imoyama Forest - for details see “{{jalink|Imoseyama (Nara)|Q112339073}}.” Designated March 24, 1928 (Shōwa 3). === Works in which it appears === * From the '{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' {{Cquote|Ōanamuchi, Sukunamikami, who made Imoseyama—how pleasing it is to see|20px||'{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' Vol.7 No.1247'Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū 7 Man'yōshū (2),' Shogakukan, 2004 ({{jalink|JapanKnowledge|Q11309996}} edition), pp. 230-231.}} * From the '{{jalink|Kokin Wakashū|Q1047167}}' {{Cquote|Flowing on and falling between Imoseyama, Yoshino River—ah, such is this world|20px||Anonymous, '{{jalink|Kokin Wakashū|Q1047167}}'{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}}} Note that some theories identify the “Imoseyama” in these poems not with this place but with Kii’s {{jalink|Imoyama/Seyama (Wakayama)|Q0|Imoyama and Seyama}} ({{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Ito District|Q1131309}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q1345594}}){{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. Also, the jōruri work {{jalink|Imoseyama Onna Teikin|Q11447158}} uses Imoyama and Seyama as its setting{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hatayoshi Nakano|authorlink=中野幡能|year=|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title={{jalink|Kokushi Daijiten (Shōwa period)|Q11420603|Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{jalink|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/699}} 'Meiji Jinja Shiryō Fuken Gōsha (Upper)'] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frames 699–700. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490301|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference="Imoyama / Seyama"|ref={{Harvid|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Ōnamochi Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}}}. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Toshikazu Miyasaka|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Kenichi Tanigawa|editor-link=谷川健一|author=Toshikazu Miyasaka|year=2000|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 4 Yamato |publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025045|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Onamochi-jinja}} * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Ōnamochi Shrine] - Nara Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22001 Ōnamochi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE "Collection of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおなもちしんしや}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōnamochi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōnamochi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōnamochi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Onamochi-jinja}} [[JA:大名持神社]] {{wikidata link|Q43594663}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ōnamochi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ōnamochi-jinja02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine buildings
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}{{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}} Kawaraya 86[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Ōnamochi Shrine] (Nara Prefecture Shrine Office). |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 38.42 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 52|経度秒 = 8.33 |ISO = JP-29 |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamochi Mitama-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suseribime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}} |神体 ={{jalink|Imoseyama|Q112620631}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-grand}})
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{jalink|October 17|Q2962}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}} |本殿 = {{jalink|Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名 = Ōnana-miya |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Imoyama (Yoshino), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View of Imoyama from the opposite bank}}{{small|Torii of Ōnamochi Shrine at mid-left. Foreground: [[Yoshino River (Nara)|Yoshino River]].}}]] '''Ōnamochi Shrine''' (おおなもちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Kawaraya, {{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}, {{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and its former {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534|shrine rank}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. Its common name is also “Ōnana-miya”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. It stands at the foot of isolated {{jalink|Imoyama (Nara)|Q112339073|Imoyama}}, near the branching point of the old {{jalink|Ise Kaidō}} and old Higashi-Kumano Kaidō{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following threeExplanation board in precincts.. * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamochi Mitama-no-Kami}} * {{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suseribime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}} In documents, the '{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}' mentions “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami,” the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' '{{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' lists “Ōnamochi Shrine,” and in the Engishiki section on temporary festivals (Myojin festivals) appears “Ōnamochi Mitama Shrine, one seat,” indicating that from ancient times the principal deity was one seat of Ōnamuchi (another name of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944}}). How Sukunabikona came to be enshrined is unclear; theories include a later enshrinement based on a poem in volume 7 of the '{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' (quoted below), or that he was a deity of Seyama on the opposite bank{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. The 'Jinja Meisaichō' lists four seats: Ōnamochi-no-Kami, Ōnamochi Mitama-no-Kami, consort deity Suseribime-no-Kami, and Sukunabikona-no-Kami{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. The shrine stands at the foot of {{jalink|Imoyama (Nara)|Q112339073|Imoyama}} (elevation 249 m[http://www.town.yoshino.nara.jp/kanko-event/meisho-kanko/shisetsu/ryumon-nakaryumon/imoyamajuso.html Imoyama forest] (Yoshino Town website).). Since Imoyama is also called “Imiyama (taboo mountain)” and a taboo tradition of not cutting trees remains, it is inferred that primitive {{jalink|animistic|Q43004}} worship of Imoyama as a {{jalink|shintai mountain|Q1595289}} was the shrine’s origin{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), the {{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025|divine rank}} of “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami” in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} was raised from Junior First Rank to Senior First Rank{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. At that time, in Yamato Province only {{jalink|Kasuga Taisha|Q714559}} had reached this highest rank; this promotion was extremely early even nationally, though the reason is not fully clear{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. One theory is that reverence rose because it stood at a key transport point connecting the capital in the {{jalink|Nara Basin|Q10942269}} to Yoshino{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' '{{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in {{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}, Yamato Province, as “Ōnamochi Shrine, Myojin-grand, Tsukinami/Ainame/Niiname,” and was ranked a {{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}} while being granted offerings at court’s {{jalink|Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161}}, {{jalink|Ainame Festival|Q11581944}}, and {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. In Engishiki temporary festivals, in the Myojin-festival section among 285 seats appears “Ōnamochi Mitama Shrine, one seat”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, it appears in 'Ōmiwa Bunshin Ruisha-shō narabi ni Fubi' as “Imose Shrine”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}}, 'Yamato-shi' records a {{jalink|jingū-ji|Q1074971}} called Daikai-ji in the precincts (site of current shrine office) and Shiobiu-chi in front of the shrine{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. Shiobiu-chi was said to spring salt water, and purification was once performed there{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In early {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, Daikai-ji was abolished; honji-buddhas and ritual implements were moved to Bukoku-ji, and the Daihannya-kyō to Unsen-ji{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), Jan. 27: from Junior First Rank to Senior First Rank ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') - written as “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami.” === Precincts === File:Onamochi-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Onamochi-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Onamochi-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office
{{small|Formerly site of Daikai-ji, a {{jalink|jingū-ji|Q1074971}}.}} File:Onamochi-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Sessha and massha === [[File:Onamochi-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Precinct shrine}}]] The following four precinct shrines are present. * Wakamiya Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. * Suijin Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}. * Konpira Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856}}. * Inari Shrine - deity: Inasō no Kami. === Cultural properties === === National Natural Monument === * Imoyama Forest - for details see “{{jalink|Imoseyama (Nara)|Q112339073}}.” Designated March 24, 1928 (Shōwa 3). === Works in which it appears === * From the '{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' {{Cquote|Ōanamuchi, Sukunamikami, who made Imoseyama—how pleasing it is to see|20px||'{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' Vol.7 No.1247'Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū 7 Man'yōshū (2),' Shogakukan, 2004 ({{jalink|JapanKnowledge|Q11309996}} edition), pp. 230-231.}} * From the '{{jalink|Kokin Wakashū|Q1047167}}' {{Cquote|Flowing on and falling between Imoseyama, Yoshino River—ah, such is this world|20px||Anonymous, '{{jalink|Kokin Wakashū|Q1047167}}'{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}}} Note that some theories identify the “Imoseyama” in these poems not with this place but with Kii’s {{jalink|Imoyama/Seyama (Wakayama)|Q0|Imoyama and Seyama}} ({{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Ito District|Q1131309}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q1345594}}){{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. Also, the jōruri work {{jalink|Imoseyama Onna Teikin|Q11447158}} uses Imoyama and Seyama as its setting{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hatayoshi Nakano|authorlink=中野幡能|year=|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title={{jalink|Kokushi Daijiten (Shōwa period)|Q11420603|Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{jalink|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/699}} 'Meiji Jinja Shiryō Fuken Gōsha (Upper)'] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frames 699–700. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490301|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference="Imoyama / Seyama"|ref={{Harvid|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Ōnamochi Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}}}. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Toshikazu Miyasaka|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Kenichi Tanigawa|editor-link=谷川健一|author=Toshikazu Miyasaka|year=2000|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 4 Yamato |publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025045|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Onamochi-jinja}} * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Ōnamochi Shrine] - Nara Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22001 Ōnamochi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE "Collection of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおなもちしんしや}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōnamochi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōnamochi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōnamochi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Onamochi-jinja}} [[JA:大名持神社]] {{wikidata link|Q43594663}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ōnamochi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ōnamochi-jinja02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine buildings
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}{{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}} Kawaraya 86[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Ōnamochi Shrine] (Nara Prefecture Shrine Office). |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 38.42 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 52|経度秒 = 8.33 |ISO = JP-29 |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamochi Mitama-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suseribime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}} |神体 ={{jalink|Imoseyama|Q112620631}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-grand}})
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{jalink|October 17|Q2962}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}} |本殿 = {{jalink|Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名 = Ōnana-miya |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Imoyama (Yoshino), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View of Imoyama from the opposite bank}}{{small|Torii of Ōnamochi Shrine at mid-left. Foreground: [[Yoshino River (Nara)|Yoshino River]].}}]] '''Ōnamochi Shrine''' (おおなもちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Kawaraya, {{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}, {{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and its former {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534|shrine rank}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. Its common name is also “Ōnana-miya”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. It stands at the foot of isolated {{jalink|Imoyama (Nara)|Q112339073|Imoyama}}, near the branching point of the old {{jalink|Ise Kaidō}} and old Higashi-Kumano Kaidō{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following threeExplanation board in precincts.. * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamochi Mitama-no-Kami}} * {{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suseribime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}} In documents, the '{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}' mentions “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami,” the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' '{{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' lists “Ōnamochi Shrine,” and in the Engishiki section on temporary festivals (Myojin festivals) appears “Ōnamochi Mitama Shrine, one seat,” indicating that from ancient times the principal deity was one seat of Ōnamuchi (another name of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944}}). How Sukunabikona came to be enshrined is unclear; theories include a later enshrinement based on a poem in volume 7 of the '{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' (quoted below), or that he was a deity of Seyama on the opposite bank{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. The 'Jinja Meisaichō' lists four seats: Ōnamochi-no-Kami, Ōnamochi Mitama-no-Kami, consort deity Suseribime-no-Kami, and Sukunabikona-no-Kami{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. The shrine stands at the foot of {{jalink|Imoyama (Nara)|Q112339073|Imoyama}} (elevation 249 m[http://www.town.yoshino.nara.jp/kanko-event/meisho-kanko/shisetsu/ryumon-nakaryumon/imoyamajuso.html Imoyama forest] (Yoshino Town website).). Since Imoyama is also called “Imiyama (taboo mountain)” and a taboo tradition of not cutting trees remains, it is inferred that primitive {{jalink|animistic|Q43004}} worship of Imoyama as a {{jalink|shintai mountain|Q1595289}} was the shrine’s origin{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), the {{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025|divine rank}} of “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami” in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} was raised from Junior First Rank to Senior First Rank{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. At that time, in Yamato Province only {{jalink|Kasuga Taisha|Q714559}} had reached this highest rank; this promotion was extremely early even nationally, though the reason is not fully clear{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. One theory is that reverence rose because it stood at a key transport point connecting the capital in the {{jalink|Nara Basin|Q10942269}} to Yoshino{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' '{{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in {{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}, Yamato Province, as “Ōnamochi Shrine, Myojin-grand, Tsukinami/Ainame/Niiname,” and was ranked a {{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}} while being granted offerings at court’s {{jalink|Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161}}, {{jalink|Ainame Festival|Q11581944}}, and {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. In Engishiki temporary festivals, in the Myojin-festival section among 285 seats appears “Ōnamochi Mitama Shrine, one seat”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, it appears in 'Ōmiwa Bunshin Ruisha-shō narabi ni Fubi' as “Imose Shrine”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}}, 'Yamato-shi' records a {{jalink|jingū-ji|Q1074971}} called Daikai-ji in the precincts (site of current shrine office) and Shiobiu-chi in front of the shrine{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. Shiobiu-chi was said to spring salt water, and purification was once performed there{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In early {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, Daikai-ji was abolished; honji-buddhas and ritual implements were moved to Bukoku-ji, and the Daihannya-kyō to Unsen-ji{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), Jan. 27: from Junior First Rank to Senior First Rank ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') - written as “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami.” === Precincts === File:Onamochi-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Onamochi-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Onamochi-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office
{{small|Formerly site of Daikai-ji, a {{jalink|jingū-ji|Q1074971}}.}} File:Onamochi-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Sessha and massha === [[File:Onamochi-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Precinct shrine}}]] The following four precinct shrines are present. * Wakamiya Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. * Suijin Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}. * Konpira Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856}}. * Inari Shrine - deity: Inasō no Kami. === Cultural properties === === National Natural Monument === * Imoyama Forest - for details see “{{jalink|Imoseyama (Nara)|Q112339073}}.” Designated March 24, 1928 (Shōwa 3). === Works in which it appears === * From the '{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' {{Cquote|Ōanamuchi, Sukunamikami, who made Imoseyama—how pleasing it is to see|20px||'{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' Vol.7 No.1247'Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū 7 Man'yōshū (2),' Shogakukan, 2004 ({{jalink|JapanKnowledge|Q11309996}} edition), pp. 230-231.}} * From the '{{jalink|Kokin Wakashū|Q1047167}}' {{Cquote|Flowing on and falling between Imoseyama, Yoshino River—ah, such is this world|20px||Anonymous, '{{jalink|Kokin Wakashū|Q1047167}}'{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}}} Note that some theories identify the “Imoseyama” in these poems not with this place but with Kii’s {{jalink|Imoyama/Seyama (Wakayama)|Q0|Imoyama and Seyama}} ({{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Ito District|Q1131309}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q1345594}}){{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. Also, the jōruri work {{jalink|Imoseyama Onna Teikin|Q11447158}} uses Imoyama and Seyama as its setting{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hatayoshi Nakano|authorlink=中野幡能|year=|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title={{jalink|Kokushi Daijiten (Shōwa period)|Q11420603|Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{jalink|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/699}} 'Meiji Jinja Shiryō Fuken Gōsha (Upper)'] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frames 699–700. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490301|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference="Imoyama / Seyama"|ref={{Harvid|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Ōnamochi Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}}}. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Toshikazu Miyasaka|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Kenichi Tanigawa|editor-link=谷川健一|author=Toshikazu Miyasaka|year=2000|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Foreground: [[Yoshino River (Nara)|Yoshino River]].}}]] '''Ōnamochi Shrine''' (おおなもちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Kawaraya, {{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}, {{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and its former {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534|shrine rank}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. Its common name is also “Ōnana-miya”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. It stands at the foot of isolated {{jalink|Imoyama (Nara)|Q112339073|Imoyama}}, near the branching point of the old {{jalink|Ise Kaidō}} and old Higashi-Kumano Kaidō{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following threeExplanation board in precincts.. * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamochi Mitama-no-Kami}} * {{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suseribime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}} In documents, the '{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}' mentions “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami,” the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' '{{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' lists “Ōnamochi Shrine,” and in the Engishiki section on temporary festivals (Myojin festivals) appears “Ōnamochi Mitama Shrine, one seat,” indicating that from ancient times the principal deity was one seat of Ōnamuchi (another name of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944}}). How Sukunabikona came to be enshrined is unclear; theories include a later enshrinement based on a poem in volume 7 of the '{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' (quoted below), or that he was a deity of Seyama on the opposite bank{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. The 'Jinja Meisaichō' lists four seats: Ōnamochi-no-Kami, Ōnamochi Mitama-no-Kami, consort deity Suseribime-no-Kami, and Sukunabikona-no-Kami{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. The shrine stands at the foot of {{jalink|Imoyama (Nara)|Q112339073|Imoyama}} (elevation 249 m[http://www.town.yoshino.nara.jp/kanko-event/meisho-kanko/shisetsu/ryumon-nakaryumon/imoyamajuso.html Imoyama forest] (Yoshino Town website).). Since Imoyama is also called “Imiyama (taboo mountain)” and a taboo tradition of not cutting trees remains, it is inferred that primitive {{jalink|animistic|Q43004}} worship of Imoyama as a {{jalink|shintai mountain|Q1595289}} was the shrine’s origin{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), the {{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025|divine rank}} of “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami” in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} was raised from Junior First Rank to Senior First Rank{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. At that time, in Yamato Province only {{jalink|Kasuga Taisha|Q714559}} had reached this highest rank; this promotion was extremely early even nationally, though the reason is not fully clear{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. One theory is that reverence rose because it stood at a key transport point connecting the capital in the {{jalink|Nara Basin|Q10942269}} to Yoshino{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' '{{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in {{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}, Yamato Province, as “Ōnamochi Shrine, Myojin-grand, Tsukinami/Ainame/Niiname,” and was ranked a {{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}} while being granted offerings at court’s {{jalink|Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161}}, {{jalink|Ainame Festival|Q11581944}}, and {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. In Engishiki temporary festivals, in the Myojin-festival section among 285 seats appears “Ōnamochi Mitama Shrine, one seat”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, it appears in 'Ōmiwa Bunshin Ruisha-shō narabi ni Fubi' as “Imose Shrine”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}}, 'Yamato-shi' records a {{jalink|jingū-ji|Q1074971}} called Daikai-ji in the precincts (site of current shrine office) and Shiobiu-chi in front of the shrine{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. Shiobiu-chi was said to spring salt water, and purification was once performed there{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In early {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, Daikai-ji was abolished; honji-buddhas and ritual implements were moved to Bukoku-ji, and the Daihannya-kyō to Unsen-ji{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), Jan. 27: from Junior First Rank to Senior First Rank ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') - written as “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami.” === Precincts === File:Onamochi-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Onamochi-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Onamochi-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office
{{small|Formerly site of Daikai-ji, a {{jalink|jingū-ji|Q1074971}}.}} File:Onamochi-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Sessha and massha === [[File:Onamochi-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Precinct shrine}}]] The following four precinct shrines are present. * Wakamiya Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. * Suijin Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}. * Konpira Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856}}. * Inari Shrine - deity: Inasō no Kami. === Cultural properties === === National Natural Monument === * Imoyama Forest - for details see “{{jalink|Imoseyama (Nara)|Q112339073}}.” Designated March 24, 1928 (Shōwa 3). === Works in which it appears === * From the '{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' {{Cquote|Ōanamuchi, Sukunamikami, who made Imoseyama—how pleasing it is to see|20px||'{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' Vol.7 No.1247'Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū 7 Man'yōshū (2),' Shogakukan, 2004 ({{jalink|JapanKnowledge|Q11309996}} edition), pp. 230-231.}} * From the '{{jalink|Kokin Wakashū|Q1047167}}' {{Cquote|Flowing on and falling between Imoseyama, Yoshino River—ah, such is this world|20px||Anonymous, '{{jalink|Kokin Wakashū|Q1047167}}'{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}}} Note that some theories identify the “Imoseyama” in these poems not with this place but with Kii’s {{jalink|Imoyama/Seyama (Wakayama)|Q0|Imoyama and Seyama}} ({{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Ito District|Q1131309}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q1345594}}){{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. Also, the jōruri work {{jalink|Imoseyama Onna Teikin|Q11447158}} uses Imoyama and Seyama as its setting{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hatayoshi Nakano|authorlink=中野幡能|year=|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title={{jalink|Kokushi Daijiten (Shōwa period)|Q11420603|Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{jalink|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/699}} 'Meiji Jinja Shiryō Fuken Gōsha (Upper)'] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frames 699–700. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490301|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference="Imoyama / Seyama"|ref={{Harvid|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Ōnamochi Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}}}. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Toshikazu Miyasaka|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Kenichi Tanigawa|editor-link=谷川健一|author=Toshikazu Miyasaka|year=2000|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 4 Yamato |publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025045|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Onamochi-jinja}} * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Ōnamochi Shrine] - Nara Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22001 Ōnamochi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE "Collection of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおなもちしんしや}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōnamochi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōnamochi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōnamochi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]]

torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Onamochi-jinja}} [[JA:大名持神社]] {{wikidata link|Q43594663}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ōnamochi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ōnamochi-jinja02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine buildings
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}{{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}} Kawaraya 86[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Ōnamochi Shrine] (Nara Prefecture Shrine Office). |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 38.42 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 52|経度秒 = 8.33 |ISO = JP-29 |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamochi Mitama-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suseribime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}} |神体 ={{jalink|Imoseyama|Q112620631}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-grand}})
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{jalink|October 17|Q2962}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}} |本殿 = {{jalink|Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名 = Ōnana-miya |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Imoyama (Yoshino), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View of Imoyama from the opposite bank}}{{small|Torii of Ōnamochi Shrine at mid-left. Foreground: [[Yoshino River (Nara)|Yoshino River]].}}]] '''Ōnamochi Shrine''' (おおなもちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Kawaraya, {{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}, {{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and its former {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534|shrine rank}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. Its common name is also “Ōnana-miya”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. It stands at the foot of isolated {{jalink|Imoyama (Nara)|Q112339073|Imoyama}}, near the branching point of the old {{jalink|Ise Kaidō}} and old Higashi-Kumano Kaidō{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following threeExplanation board in precincts.. * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamochi Mitama-no-Kami}} * {{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suseribime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}} In documents, the '{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}' mentions “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami,” the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' '{{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' lists “Ōnamochi Shrine,” and in the Engishiki section on temporary festivals (Myojin festivals) appears “Ōnamochi Mitama Shrine, one seat,” indicating that from ancient times the principal deity was one seat of Ōnamuchi (another name of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944}}). How Sukunabikona came to be enshrined is unclear; theories include a later enshrinement based on a poem in volume 7 of the '{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' (quoted below), or that he was a deity of Seyama on the opposite bank{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. The 'Jinja Meisaichō' lists four seats: Ōnamochi-no-Kami, Ōnamochi Mitama-no-Kami, consort deity Suseribime-no-Kami, and Sukunabikona-no-Kami{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. The shrine stands at the foot of {{jalink|Imoyama (Nara)|Q112339073|Imoyama}} (elevation 249 m[http://www.town.yoshino.nara.jp/kanko-event/meisho-kanko/shisetsu/ryumon-nakaryumon/imoyamajuso.html Imoyama forest] (Yoshino Town website).). Since Imoyama is also called “Imiyama (taboo mountain)” and a taboo tradition of not cutting trees remains, it is inferred that primitive {{jalink|animistic|Q43004}} worship of Imoyama as a {{jalink|shintai mountain|Q1595289}} was the shrine’s origin{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), the {{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025|divine rank}} of “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami” in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} was raised from Junior First Rank to Senior First Rank{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. At that time, in Yamato Province only {{jalink|Kasuga Taisha|Q714559}} had reached this highest rank; this promotion was extremely early even nationally, though the reason is not fully clear{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. One theory is that reverence rose because it stood at a key transport point connecting the capital in the {{jalink|Nara Basin|Q10942269}} to Yoshino{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' '{{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in {{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}, Yamato Province, as “Ōnamochi Shrine, Myojin-grand, Tsukinami/Ainame/Niiname,” and was ranked a {{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}} while being granted offerings at court’s {{jalink|Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161}}, {{jalink|Ainame Festival|Q11581944}}, and {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. In Engishiki temporary festivals, in the Myojin-festival section among 285 seats appears “Ōnamochi Mitama Shrine, one seat”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, it appears in 'Ōmiwa Bunshin Ruisha-shō narabi ni Fubi' as “Imose Shrine”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}}, 'Yamato-shi' records a {{jalink|jingū-ji|Q1074971}} called Daikai-ji in the precincts (site of current shrine office) and Shiobiu-chi in front of the shrine{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. Shiobiu-chi was said to spring salt water, and purification was once performed there{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In early {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, Daikai-ji was abolished; honji-buddhas and ritual implements were moved to Bukoku-ji, and the Daihannya-kyō to Unsen-ji{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), Jan. 27: from Junior First Rank to Senior First Rank ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') - written as “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami.” === Precincts === File:Onamochi-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Onamochi-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Onamochi-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office
{{small|Formerly site of Daikai-ji, a {{jalink|jingū-ji|Q1074971}}.}} File:Onamochi-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Sessha and massha === [[File:Onamochi-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Precinct shrine}}]] The following four precinct shrines are present. * Wakamiya Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. * Suijin Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}. * Konpira Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856}}. * Inari Shrine - deity: Inasō no Kami. === Cultural properties === === National Natural Monument === * Imoyama Forest - for details see “{{jalink|Imoseyama (Nara)|Q112339073}}.” Designated March 24, 1928 (Shōwa 3). === Works in which it appears === * From the '{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' {{Cquote|Ōanamuchi, Sukunamikami, who made Imoseyama—how pleasing it is to see|20px||'{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' Vol.7 No.1247'Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū 7 Man'yōshū (2),' Shogakukan, 2004 ({{jalink|JapanKnowledge|Q11309996}} edition), pp. 230-231.}} * From the '{{jalink|Kokin Wakashū|Q1047167}}' {{Cquote|Flowing on and falling between Imoseyama, Yoshino River—ah, such is this world|20px||Anonymous, '{{jalink|Kokin Wakashū|Q1047167}}'{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}}} Note that some theories identify the “Imoseyama” in these poems not with this place but with Kii’s {{jalink|Imoyama/Seyama (Wakayama)|Q0|Imoyama and Seyama}} ({{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Ito District|Q1131309}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q1345594}}){{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. Also, the jōruri work {{jalink|Imoseyama Onna Teikin|Q11447158}} uses Imoyama and Seyama as its setting{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hatayoshi Nakano|authorlink=中野幡能|year=|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title={{jalink|Kokushi Daijiten (Shōwa period)|Q11420603|Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{jalink|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/699}} 'Meiji Jinja Shiryō Fuken Gōsha (Upper)'] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frames 699–700. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490301|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference="Imoyama / Seyama"|ref={{Harvid|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Ōnamochi Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}}}. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Toshikazu Miyasaka|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Kenichi Tanigawa|editor-link=谷川健一|author=Toshikazu Miyasaka|year=2000|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 4 Yamato |publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025045|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Onamochi-jinja}} * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Ōnamochi Shrine] - Nara Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22001 Ōnamochi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE "Collection of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおなもちしんしや}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōnamochi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōnamochi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōnamochi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine buildings
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}{{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}} Kawaraya 86[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Ōnamochi Shrine] (Nara Prefecture Shrine Office). |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 38.42 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 52|経度秒 = 8.33 |ISO = JP-29 |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamochi Mitama-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suseribime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}} |神体 ={{jalink|Imoseyama|Q112620631}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-grand}})
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{jalink|October 17|Q2962}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}} |本殿 = {{jalink|Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名 = Ōnana-miya |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Imoyama (Yoshino), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View of Imoyama from the opposite bank}}{{small|Torii of Ōnamochi Shrine at mid-left. Foreground: [[Yoshino River (Nara)|Yoshino River]].}}]] '''Ōnamochi Shrine''' (おおなもちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Kawaraya, {{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}, {{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and its former {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534|shrine rank}} was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. Its common name is also “Ōnana-miya”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. It stands at the foot of isolated {{jalink|Imoyama (Nara)|Q112339073|Imoyama}}, near the branching point of the old {{jalink|Ise Kaidō}} and old Higashi-Kumano Kaidō{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following threeExplanation board in precincts.. * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamochi Mitama-no-Kami}} * {{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suseribime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}} In documents, the '{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}' mentions “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami,” the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' '{{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' lists “Ōnamochi Shrine,” and in the Engishiki section on temporary festivals (Myojin festivals) appears “Ōnamochi Mitama Shrine, one seat,” indicating that from ancient times the principal deity was one seat of Ōnamuchi (another name of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944}}). How Sukunabikona came to be enshrined is unclear; theories include a later enshrinement based on a poem in volume 7 of the '{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' (quoted below), or that he was a deity of Seyama on the opposite bank{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. The 'Jinja Meisaichō' lists four seats: Ōnamochi-no-Kami, Ōnamochi Mitama-no-Kami, consort deity Suseribime-no-Kami, and Sukunabikona-no-Kami{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. The shrine stands at the foot of {{jalink|Imoyama (Nara)|Q112339073|Imoyama}} (elevation 249 m[http://www.town.yoshino.nara.jp/kanko-event/meisho-kanko/shisetsu/ryumon-nakaryumon/imoyamajuso.html Imoyama forest] (Yoshino Town website).). Since Imoyama is also called “Imiyama (taboo mountain)” and a taboo tradition of not cutting trees remains, it is inferred that primitive {{jalink|animistic|Q43004}} worship of Imoyama as a {{jalink|shintai mountain|Q1595289}} was the shrine’s origin{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), the {{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025|divine rank}} of “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami” in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} was raised from Junior First Rank to Senior First Rank{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. At that time, in Yamato Province only {{jalink|Kasuga Taisha|Q714559}} had reached this highest rank; this promotion was extremely early even nationally, though the reason is not fully clear{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. One theory is that reverence rose because it stood at a key transport point connecting the capital in the {{jalink|Nara Basin|Q10942269}} to Yoshino{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' '{{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it is listed in {{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}, Yamato Province, as “Ōnamochi Shrine, Myojin-grand, Tsukinami/Ainame/Niiname,” and was ranked a {{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}} while being granted offerings at court’s {{jalink|Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161}}, {{jalink|Ainame Festival|Q11581944}}, and {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}. In Engishiki temporary festivals, in the Myojin-festival section among 285 seats appears “Ōnamochi Mitama Shrine, one seat”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, it appears in 'Ōmiwa Bunshin Ruisha-shō narabi ni Fubi' as “Imose Shrine”{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}}, 'Yamato-shi' records a {{jalink|jingū-ji|Q1074971}} called Daikai-ji in the precincts (site of current shrine office) and Shiobiu-chi in front of the shrine{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}. Shiobiu-chi was said to spring salt water, and purification was once performed there{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. In early {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, Daikai-ji was abolished; honji-buddhas and ritual implements were moved to Bukoku-ji, and the Daihannya-kyō to Unsen-ji{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), Jan. 27: from Junior First Rank to Senior First Rank ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') - written as “Ōanamuchi-no-Kami.” === Precincts === File:Onamochi-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Onamochi-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Onamochi-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office
{{small|Formerly site of Daikai-ji, a {{jalink|jingū-ji|Q1074971}}.}} File:Onamochi-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Sessha and massha === [[File:Onamochi-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Precinct shrine}}]] The following four precinct shrines are present. * Wakamiya Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. * Suijin Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Mizuhanome-no-Kami}}. * Konpira Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856}}. * Inari Shrine - deity: Inasō no Kami. === Cultural properties === === National Natural Monument === * Imoyama Forest - for details see “{{jalink|Imoseyama (Nara)|Q112339073}}.” Designated March 24, 1928 (Shōwa 3). === Works in which it appears === * From the '{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' {{Cquote|Ōanamuchi, Sukunamikami, who made Imoseyama—how pleasing it is to see|20px||'{{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}}' Vol.7 No.1247'Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū 7 Man'yōshū (2),' Shogakukan, 2004 ({{jalink|JapanKnowledge|Q11309996}} edition), pp. 230-231.}} * From the '{{jalink|Kokin Wakashū|Q1047167}}' {{Cquote|Flowing on and falling between Imoseyama, Yoshino River—ah, such is this world|20px||Anonymous, '{{jalink|Kokin Wakashū|Q1047167}}'{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}}} Note that some theories identify the “Imoseyama” in these poems not with this place but with Kii’s {{jalink|Imoyama/Seyama (Wakayama)|Q0|Imoyama and Seyama}} ({{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Ito District|Q1131309}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q1345594}}){{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. Also, the jōruri work {{jalink|Imoseyama Onna Teikin|Q11447158}} uses Imoyama and Seyama as its setting{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hatayoshi Nakano|authorlink=中野幡能|year=|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title={{jalink|Kokushi Daijiten (Shōwa period)|Q11420603|Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{jalink|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/699}} 'Meiji Jinja Shiryō Fuken Gōsha (Upper)'] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frames 699–700. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 30 Place Names of Nara Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490301|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference="Imoyama / Seyama"|ref={{Harvid|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Ōnamochi Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}}}. * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Toshikazu Miyasaka|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|year=1982|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.2|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Kenichi Tanigawa|editor-link=谷川健一|author=Toshikazu Miyasaka|year=2000|chapter=Ōnamochi Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 4 Yamato |publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025045|ref={{Harvid|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Onamochi-jinja}} * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Ōnamochi Shrine] - Nara Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22001 Ōnamochi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE "Collection of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおなもちしんしや}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōnamochi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōnamochi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōnamochi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Onamochi-jinja}} [[JA:大名持神社]] {{wikidata link|Q43594663}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 大名持神社 |画像 = [[File:Ōnamochi-jinja02.jpg|270px]]
社殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|吉野郡|Q1154558}}{{jalink|吉野町|Q1203731}}河原屋86[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 大名持神社]（奈良県神社庁）。 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 38.42 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 52|経度秒 = 8.33 |ISO = JP-29 |祭神 = {{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大名持御魂神}}
{{jalink|スセリビメ|Q8191715|須勢理比咩命}}
{{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少名彦名命}} |神体 ={{jalink|妹背山|Q112620631}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}）
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = {{jalink|10月17日|Q2962}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}} |本殿 = {{jalink|神明造|Q930545}} |別名 = 大汝宮 |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Imoyama (Yoshino), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|対岸から妹山を望む}}{{small|中左に大名持神社鳥居。手前は[[吉野川 (奈良県)|吉野川]]。}}]] '''大名持神社'''（おおなもちじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|吉野郡|Q1154558}}{{jalink|吉野町|Q1203731}}河原屋にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。通称を「大汝宮」とも{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 旧{{jalink|伊勢街道}}・旧東熊野街道の分岐点付近、独立峰の{{jalink|妹背山 (奈良県)|Q112339073|妹山}}の山麓に鎮座する{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の3柱境内説明板。。 * {{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大名持御魂神}} * {{jalink|スセリビメ|Q8191715|須勢理比咩命}} * {{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少名彦名命}} 文献では『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』に「大己貴神」、『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}に「大名持神社」、『延喜式』臨時祭 名神祭に「大名持御魂神社一座」と見え、古くから祭神をオオナムチ（オオクニヌシ（{{jalink|大国主|Q276944}}）の別名）の1座とする。 スクナビコナ（少名彦名）も祭神に祀られる経緯は詳らかでなく、『{{jalink|万葉集|Q834321}}』巻7の歌（後掲）に因んでの後世の合祀とする説や、対岸の背山の神とする説がある{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}。なお、『神社明細帳』では祭神を大名持神・大名持御魂神・后神須勢理比咩神・少名彦名神の4座とする{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}。 === 歴史 === === 概史 === 創建は不詳。{{jalink|妹背山 (奈良県)|Q112339073|妹山}}（標高249メートル[http://www.town.yoshino.nara.jp/kanko-event/meisho-kanko/shisetsu/ryumon-nakaryumon/imoyamajuso.html 妹山樹叢]（吉野町ホームページ）。）の麓に鎮座し、妹山は「忌山（いみやま）」とも称されて現在まで木を伐らない禁忌信仰が残ることから、妹山を{{jalink|神体山|Q1595289}}とする{{jalink|アニミズム|Q43004|原始信仰}}が創祀になると推測される{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}。 {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}では、{{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）に{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}の「大己貴神」の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}が従一位から正一位に昇叙された旨が記される{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}。当時に正一位の極位に達していたのは大和国では{{jalink|春日大社|Q714559}}のみで、全国的にも極めて早い昇叙であるが、その理由は必ずしも詳らかでない{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}。一説には、{{jalink|奈良盆地|Q10942269}}の宮都から吉野へとつながる交通上の要衝であったことで崇敬を高めたと推測される{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では、大和国{{jalink|吉野郡|Q1154558}}に「大名持神社 名神大 月次相嘗新嘗」として、{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}に列するとともに朝廷の{{jalink|月次祭|Q11516161}}・{{jalink|相嘗祭|Q11581944}}・{{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}}では幣帛に預かる旨が定められている{{Sfn|大名持神社（式内社）|1982}}。また『延喜式』臨時祭のうち、名神祭条では名神祭二百八十五座のうちに「大名持御魂神社一座」が見える{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 {{jalink|中世|Q12554}}期には、『大神分神類社鈔並附尾』に「妹背神社」と見える{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 {{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}期の『大和志』では、境内に{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}の大海寺（現在の社務所の場所）、社前に潮生淵があると見える{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|大名持神社（神々）|2000}}。潮生淵は潮水が湧くといわれ、かつては同地で禊も行われたという{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列した{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}初年に大海寺は廃され、本地仏・仏具は仏国寺に、大般若経は運川寺に移されている{{Sfn|大名持神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）1月27日、従一位から正一位 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「大己貴神」。 === 境内 === File:Onamochi-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Onamochi-jinja, haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Onamochi-jinja, shamusho.jpg|社務所
{{small|古くは{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}の大海寺が所在。}} File:Onamochi-jinja, torii.jpg|鳥居 === 摂末社 === [[File:Onamochi-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|境内社}}]] 境内社として次の4社がある。 * 若宮神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|事代主神}}。 * 水神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|ミヅハノメ|Q8191796|罔象女神}}。 * 金比羅神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856}}。 * 稲荷神社 - 祭神：稲蒼神。 === 文化財 === === 国の天然記念物 === * 妹山樹叢 - 詳細は「{{jalink|妹背山 (奈良県)|Q112339073}}」を参照。1928年（昭和3年）3月24日指定。 === 登場作品 === * 『{{jalink|万葉集|Q834321}}』より {{Cquote|大穴道（おほあなみち）　少御神（すくなみかみ）の　作らしし　妹背の山を　見らくし良よしも|20px||『{{jalink|万葉集|Q834321}}』巻7 1247番『新編日本古典文学全集 7 萬葉集 (2)』小学館、2004年（{{jalink|ジャパンナレッジ|Q11309996}}版）、pp. 230-231。}} * 『{{jalink|古今和歌集|Q1047167}}』より {{Cquote|流れては　いもせのやまの　なかにおつる　吉野の河の　よしや世中|20px||読人しらず、『{{jalink|古今和歌集|Q1047167}}』{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}}} なおこれらの歌に見える「妹背山」を、当地でなく紀伊の{{jalink|妹背山 (和歌山県)||妹山・背山}}（{{jalink|和歌山県|Q131314}}{{jalink|伊都郡|Q1131309}}{{jalink|かつらぎ町|Q1345594}}）に比定する説もある{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}。また妹山・背山を舞台とする作品として、{{jalink|浄瑠璃|Q1361891}}の「{{jalink|妹背山婦女庭訓|Q11447158}}」がある{{Sfn|妹山・背山（平凡社）|1981}}。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=中野幡能|authorlink=中野幡能|year=|chapter=大名持神社|title={{jalink|国史大辞典 torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Onamochi-jinja}} [[JA:大名持神社]] {{wikidata link|Q43594663}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Daimamochi Shrine |Image = [[File:Ōnamochi-jinja02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}{{jalink|Yoshino Town|Q1203731}} Kawaraya 86[http://www .narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Daimamochi Shrine] (Nara Prefectural Shrine Agency). |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 23|Latitude seconds = 38.42 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 52|Longitude seconds = 8.33 |ISO = JP-29 |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Daimyomochimitamokami}}
{{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suserihime no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Shonahikona no Mikoto}} |Divinity ={{jalink|Imoseyama|Q112620631}}|Company status = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}})
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{jalink|October 17th|Q2962}}{{Sfn|Daimamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}} |Main shrine = {{jalink|Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |Alias = Daigomiya |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Imoyama (Yoshino), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View of Imosayama from the opposite bank}}{{small|The torii gate of Daimamochi Shrine is on the center left. In the foreground is [[Yoshino River (Nara Prefecture)|Yoshino River]]. }}]] ``Dainamochi Shrine'' is located in Kawaraya, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}{{jalink|Yoshino Town|Q1203731}}. It is {{jalink|Shikiuchi}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. It is also commonly known as ``Daigo Shrine''.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}} is located near the junction of the former {{jalink|Ise Kaido}} and the former Higashi-Kumano Kaido, at the foot of the independent peak {{jalink|Mt. Imose (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|Imoseyama}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. . * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Daimyochimitamokami}} * {{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suserihime no Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Shonahikona no Mikoto}} In the literature, there is ``Daimomichi Shrine'' in ``{{jalink|Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981}}'', ``Dainamochi Shrine'' in ``{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'', and ``Engishiki'' extraordinary festival. It is seen as the ``Daimyochimitama Shrine Troupe'' in the Meishin Festival, and the enshrined deity has been one of the troupes of Onamuchi (another name for Okuninushi ({{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944}})). It is not clear how Sukunabikona (Shona Hikona) was enshrined as a deity, but it was later named after a poem in volume 7 of "{{jalink|Manyoshu|Q834321}}" (see below). There is a theory that it is a joint enshrinement of the gods, and another that it is the god of the mountain on the opposite bank. Furthermore, in the "Shinja Detailed Book", there are four deities enshrined: Daimyomochi-kami, Daimyochimitama-kami, the concubine Suserihime-kami, and Shunahikomakami. === History === === Outline === Founding date is unknown. {{jalink|Shisetsu (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|Imoyamajuso}} (249 meters above sea level[http://www.town.yoshino.nara.jp/kanko-event/meisho-kanko/shisetsu/ryumon-nakaryumon/imoyamajuso.html Imosayama is also called ``Imiyama,'' and it is said to be a sacred mountain because there is a taboo belief against cutting down trees to this day. k|Shintaiyama|Q1595289}} {{jalink|animism|Q43004|primitive faith}} is presumed to be the founding shrine {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (gods)|2000}}.In {{jalink|Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokukushi}}, {{jalink|Cheonan (Japan)|Q1075611|In the year 3 of Tenan}} ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}'s ``Great God'' |Shinkai|Q11591025}} is recorded to the effect that he was promoted from Juichii to Shoichii. At that time, only {{jalink|Kasuga Taisha|Q714559}} in Yamato Province reached the highest rank of Shoichii, which was an extremely early promotion nationwide, but the reason for this is not necessarily clear {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1982}}{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. One theory is that it was highly revered because it was an important transportation hub connecting the imperial capital of {{jalink|Nara Basin|Q10942269}} to Yoshino {{Sfn|Isenyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}.{{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} 5 years ({{jalink|927 years|Q30470}}) “{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448 }}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, in Yamato Province {{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}, ``Daimamochi Shrine Meishin University As a "Monthly Aijo Niiname", it is listed at {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} and the {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink| It is stipulated that at the Niinamesai|Q11581944}} and {{jalink|Ninamesai|Q11501518}}, it is kept in a heihaku. Also, among the ``Engishiki'' special festivals, the ``Daimyo Mochimitama Shrine Troupe'' can be seen among the 285 Meishin Festivals in the Meishin Festival. In the {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}} period, ``Imose Shrine'' can be seen in ``Okami Bunshin Rui Shrine Tsunami Tsukio'' {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}} period, ``Yamatoshi'' shows {{jalink|Jinguji|Q1074971}} Daikai-ji Temple (the location of the current shrine office) in the precincts, and a tidal pool in front of the shrine.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. It is said that the tide water gushes out from the Choobuchi, and purification was once performed there {{Sfn|Daimamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}.After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} in {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}In the first year of the Meiji era, Daikai-ji Temple was abolished, the original Buddha and Buddhist utensils were moved to Bukkoku-ji Temple, and the Daihanya Sutra was moved to Unsen-ji Temple.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. === God level === * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from Juichii to Shoichii ('{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') - Written as 'Omi Kikami'. === Precincts === File:Onamochi-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamochi-jinja, haiden.jpg|House of worship File:Onamochi-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office
{{small|Oldly located at Daikaiji Temple, {{jalink|Jinguji|Q1074971}}. }} File:Onamochi-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === [[File:Onamochi-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shinuchisha}}]] There are the following four companies within the precincts . * Wakamiya Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Kotoshirosu|Q8009611|Kotoshirosu no Kami}}. * Water Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Mizuhanome|Q8191796|Elephant Goddess}}. * Konpira Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856}}. * Inari Shrine - Enshrined deity: Inasoujin. === Cultural assets === === National Natural Monument === * Imoseyama Trees - For details, see "{{jalink|Imoseyama (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073}}". Designated on March 24, 1928 . === Appeared works === * From “{{jalink|Manyoshu|Q834321}}” {{Cquote|Ohoanamichi, Sukunamikami's Shishi Shishi, Imose no Mt.|20px||"{{jalink|Man'yoshu|Q834321}}" Volume 7 No. 1247"New Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 7 Man'yoshu (2)” Shogakukan, 2004 ({{jalink|Japan Knowledge|Q11309996}} edition), pp. 230-231. }} * From “{{jalink|Kokin Wakashu|Q1047167}}” {{Cquote|Flowing and climbing in the mountains of Imose, Yoshiya world of the Yoshino river|20px||Unknown reader, "{{jalink|Kokin Wakashu|Q1047167}}" {{Sfn|Seyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} It should be noted that the ``Mt. Imose'' that appears in these songs is not from here, but from Mt. Imose in Kii. (Wakayama Prefecture)||Seyama/Seyama}} ({{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Ito District|Q1131309}}{{jalink|Katsuragi Town|Q1345594}}) There is also a theory that it is compared to {{Sfn|Seyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Another work set in Seyama and Seyama is {{jalink|Joruri|Q1361891}}'s ``{{jalink|Imoseyama Women's Teikun|Q11447158}}'' {{Sfn|Seyama and Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} book|Japanese book|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Daimamochi Shrine|title={{jalink|Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine
