torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Onamochi-jinja}} [[JA:大名持神社]] {{wikidata link|Q43594663}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Daimamochi Shrine |Image = [[File:Ōnamochi-jinja02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino Town|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731}} Kawaraya 86[http://www .narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Daimamochi Shrine] (Nara Prefectural Shrine Agency). |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 23|Latitude seconds = 38.42 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 52|Longitude seconds = 8.33 |ISO = JP-29 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Daimyomochimitamokami}}
{{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Shonahikona no Mikoto}} |Divinity ={{ill|lt=Imoseyama|Q112620631|WD=Q112620631}}|Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University}})
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 17th|Q2962|WD=Q2962}}{{Sfn|Daimamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}} |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} |Alias = Daigomiya |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Imoyama (Yoshino), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View of Imosayama from the opposite bank}}{{small|The torii gate of Daimamochi Shrine is on the center left. In the foreground is [[Yoshino River (Nara Prefecture)|Yoshino River]]. }}]] ``Dainamochi Shrine'' is located in Kawaraya, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino Town|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731}}. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is also commonly known as ``Daigo Shrine''.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}} is located near the junction of the former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ise Kaido|1=UNKNOWN}} and the former Higashi-Kumano Kaido, at the foot of the independent peak {{ill|lt=Mt. Imose (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|lt=Imoseyama}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. . * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Daimyochimitamokami}} * {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Shonahikona no Mikoto}} In the literature, there is ``Daimomichi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'', ``Dainamochi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'', and ``Engishiki'' extraordinary festival. It is seen as the ``Daimyochimitama Shrine Troupe'' in the Meishin Festival, and the enshrined deity has been one of the troupes of Onamuchi (another name for Okuninushi ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}})). It is not clear how Sukunabikona (Shona Hikona) was enshrined as a deity, but it was later named after a poem in volume 7 of "{{ill|lt=Manyoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321}}" (see below). There is a theory that it is a joint enshrinement of the gods, and another that it is the god of the mountain on the opposite bank. Furthermore, in the "Shinja Detailed Book", there are four deities enshrined: Daimyomochi-kami, Daimyochimitama-kami, the concubine Suserihime-kami, and Shunahikomakami. === History === === Outline === Founding date is unknown. {{ill|lt=Shisetsu (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|lt=Imoyamajuso}} (249 meters above sea level[http://www.town.yoshino.nara.jp/kanko-event/meisho-kanko/shisetsu/ryumon-nakaryumon/imoyamajuso.html Imosayama is also called ``Imiyama,'' and it is said to be a sacred mountain because there is a taboo belief against cutting down trees to this day. k|Shintaiyama|Q1595289}} {{ill|lt=animism|Q43004|WD=Q43004|lt=primitive faith}} is presumed to be the founding shrine {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (gods)|2000}}.In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokukushi}}, {{ill|lt=Cheonan (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=In the year 3 of Tenan}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}'s ``Great God'' |Shinkai|Q11591025}} is recorded to the effect that he was promoted from Juichii to Shoichii. At that time, only {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}} in Yamato Province reached the highest rank of Shoichii, which was an extremely early promotion nationwide, but the reason for this is not necessarily clear {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1982}}{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. One theory is that it was highly revered because it was an important transportation hub connecting the imperial capital of {{ill|lt=Nara Basin|Q10942269|WD=Q10942269}} to Yoshino {{Sfn|Isenyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) “{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}, in Yamato Province {{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558}}, ``Daimamochi Shrine Meishin University As a "Monthly Aijo Niiname", it is listed at {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and the {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}} and {{ill|lt=It is stipulated that at the Niinamesai|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944}} and {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}}, it is kept in a heihaku. Also, among the ``Engishiki'' special festivals, the ``Daimyo Mochimitama Shrine Troupe'' can be seen among the 285 Meishin Festivals in the Meishin Festival. In the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} period, ``Imose Shrine'' can be seen in ``Okami Bunshin Rui Shrine Tsunami Tsukio'' {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} period, ``Yamatoshi'' shows {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}} Daikai-ji Temple (the location of the current shrine office) in the precincts, and a tidal pool in front of the shrine.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. It is said that the tide water gushes out from the Choobuchi, and purification was once performed there {{Sfn|Daimamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}.After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}In the first year of the Meiji era, Daikai-ji Temple was abolished, the original Buddha and Buddhist utensils were moved to Bukkoku-ji Temple, and the Daihanya Sutra was moved to Unsen-ji Temple.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), from Juichii to Shoichii ('{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}') - Written as 'Omi Kikami'. === Precincts === File:Onamochi-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamochi-jinja, haiden.jpg|House of worship File:Onamochi-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office
{{small|Oldly located at Daikaiji Temple, {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}. }} File:Onamochi-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === [[File:Onamochi-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shinuchisha}}]] There are the following four companies within the precincts . * Wakamiya Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Kotoshirosu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshirosu no Kami}}. * Water Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Elephant Goddess}}. * Konpira Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856}}. * Inari Shrine - Enshrined deity: Inasoujin. === Cultural assets === === National Natural Monument === * Imoseyama Trees - For details, see "{{ill|lt=Imoseyama (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073}}". Designated on March 24, 1928 . === Appeared works === * From “{{ill|lt=Manyoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321}}” {{Cquote|Ohoanamichi, Sukunamikami's Shishi Shishi, Imose no Mt.|20px||"{{ill|lt=Man'yoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321}}" Volume 7 No. 1247"New Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 7 Man'yoshu (2)” Shogakukan, 2004 ({{ill|lt=Japan Knowledge|Q11309996|WD=Q11309996}} edition), pp. 230-231. }} * From “{{ill|lt=Kokin Wakashu|Q1047167|WD=Q1047167}}” {{Cquote|Flowing and climbing in the mountains of Imose, Yoshiya world of the Yoshino river|20px||Unknown reader, "{{ill|lt=Kokin Wakashu|Q1047167|WD=Q1047167}}" {{Sfn|Seyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} It should be noted that the ``Mt. Imose'' that appears in these songs is not from here, but from Mt. Imose in Kii. (Wakayama Prefecture)||Seyama/Seyama}} ({{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314}}{{ill|lt=Ito District|Q1131309|WD=Q1131309}}{{ill|lt=Katsuragi Town|Q1345594|WD=Q1345594}}) There is also a theory that it is compared to {{Sfn|Seyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Another work set in Seyama and Seyama is {{ill|lt=Joruri|Q1361891|WD=Q1361891}}'s ``{{ill|lt=Imoseyama Women's Teikun|Q11447158|WD=Q11447158}}'' {{Sfn|Seyama and Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}. === torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Onamochi-jinja}} [[JA:大名持神社]] {{wikidata link|Q43594663}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Daimamochi Shrine |Image = [[File:Ōnamochi-jinja02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino Town|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731}} Kawaraya 86[http://www .narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Daimamochi Shrine] (Nara Prefectural Shrine Agency). |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 23|Latitude seconds = 38.42 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 52|Longitude seconds = 8.33 |ISO = JP-29 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Daimyomochimitamokami}}
{{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Shonahikona no Mikoto}} |Divinity ={{ill|lt=Imoseyama|Q112620631|WD=Q112620631}}|Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University}})
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 17th|Q2962|WD=Q2962}}{{Sfn|Daimamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}} |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} |Alias = Daigomiya |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Imoyama (Yoshino), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View of Imosayama from the opposite bank}}{{small|The torii gate of Daimamochi Shrine is on the center left. In the foreground is [[Yoshino River (Nara Prefecture)|Yoshino River]]. }}]] ``Dainamochi Shrine'' is located in Kawaraya, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino Town|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731}}. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is also commonly known as ``Daigo Shrine''.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}} is located near the junction of the former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ise Kaido|1=UNKNOWN}} and the former Higashi-Kumano Kaido, at the foot of the independent peak {{ill|lt=Mt. Imose (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|lt=Imoseyama}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. . * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Daimyochimitamokami}} * {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Shonahikona no Mikoto}} In the literature, there is ``Daimomichi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'', ``Dainamochi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'', and ``Engishiki'' extraordinary festival. It is seen as the ``Daimyochimitama Shrine Troupe'' in the Meishin Festival, and the enshrined deity has been one of the troupes of Onamuchi (another name for Okuninushi ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}})). It is not clear how Sukunabikona (Shona Hikona) was enshrined as a deity, but it was later named after a poem in volume 7 of "{{ill|lt=Manyoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321}}" (see below). There is a theory that it is a joint enshrinement of the gods, and another that it is the god of the mountain on the opposite bank. Furthermore, in the "Shinja Detailed Book", there are four deities enshrined: Daimyomochi-kami, Daimyochimitama-kami, the concubine Suserihime-kami, and Shunahikomakami. === History === === Outline === Founding date is unknown. {{ill|lt=Shisetsu (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|lt=Imoyamajuso}} (249 meters above sea level[http://www.town.yoshino.nara.jp/kanko-event/meisho-kanko/shisetsu/ryumon-nakaryumon/imoyamajuso.html Imosayama is also called ``Imiyama,'' and it is said to be a sacred mountain because there is a taboo belief against cutting down trees to this day. k|Shintaiyama|Q1595289}} {{ill|lt=animism|Q43004|WD=Q43004|lt=primitive faith}} is presumed to be the founding shrine {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (gods)|2000}}.In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokukushi}}, {{ill|lt=Cheonan (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=In the year 3 of Tenan}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}'s ``Great God'' |Shinkai|Q11591025}} is recorded to the effect that he was promoted from Juichii to Shoichii. At that time, only {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}} in Yamato Province reached the highest rank of Shoichii, which was an extremely early promotion nationwide, but the reason for this is not necessarily clear {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1982}}{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. One theory is that it was highly revered because it was an important transportation hub connecting the imperial capital of {{ill|lt=Nara Basin|Q10942269|WD=Q10942269}} to Yoshino {{Sfn|Isenyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) “{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}, in Yamato Province {{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558}}, ``Daimamochi Shrine Meishin University As a "Monthly Aijo Niiname", it is listed at {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and the {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}} and {{ill|lt=It is stipulated that at the Niinamesai|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944}} and {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}}, it is kept in a heihaku. Also, among the ``Engishiki'' special festivals, the ``Daimyo Mochimitama Shrine Troupe'' can be seen among the 285 Meishin Festivals in the Meishin Festival. In the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} period, ``Imose Shrine'' can be seen in ``Okami Bunshin Rui Shrine Tsunami Tsukio'' {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} period, ``Yamatoshi'' shows {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}} Daikai-ji Temple (the location of the current shrine office) in the precincts, and a tidal pool in front of the shrine.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. It is said that the tide water gushes out from the Choobuchi, and purification was once performed there {{Sfn|Daimamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}.After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}In the first year of the Meiji era, Daikai-ji Temple was abolished, the original Buddha and Buddhist utensils were moved to Bukkoku-ji Temple, and the Daihanya Sutra was moved to Unsen-ji Temple.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), from Juichii to Shoichii ('{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}') - Written as 'Omi Kikami'. === Precincts === File:Onamochi-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamochi-jinja, haiden.jpg|House of worship File:Onamochi-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office
{{small|Oldly located at Daikaiji Temple, {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}. }} File:Onamochi-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === [[File:Onamochi-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shinuchisha}}]] There are the following four companies within the precincts . * Wakamiya Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Kotoshirosu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshirosu no Kami}}. * Water Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Elephant Goddess}}. * Konpira Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856}}. * Inari Shrine - Enshrined deity: Inasoujin. === Cultural assets === === National Natural Monument === * Imoseyama Trees - For details, see "{{ill|lt=Imoseyama (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073}}". Designated on March 24, 1928 . === Appeared works === * From “{{ill|lt=Manyoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321}}” {{Cquote|Ohoanamichi, Sukunamikami's Shishi Shishi, Imose no Mt.|20px||"{{ill|lt=Man'yoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321}}" Volume 7 No. 1247"New Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 7 Man'yoshu (2)” Shogakukan, 2004 ({{ill|lt=Japan Knowledge|Q11309996|WD=Q11309996}} edition), pp. 230-231. }} * From “{{ill|lt=Kokin Wakashu|Q1047167|WD=Q1047167}}” {{Cquote|Flowing and climbing in the mountains of Imose, Yoshiya world of the Yoshino river|20px||Unknown reader, "{{ill|lt=Kokin Wakashu|Q1047167|WD=Q1047167}}" {{Sfn|Seyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} It should be noted that the ``Mt. Imose'' that appears in these songs is not from here, but from Mt. Imose in Kii. (Wakayama Prefecture)||Seyama/Seyama}} ({{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314}}{{ill|lt=Ito District|Q1131309|WD=Q1131309}}{{ill|lt=Katsuragi Town|Q1345594|WD=Q1345594}}) There is also a theory that it is compared to {{Sfn|Seyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Another work set in Seyama and Seyama is {{ill|lt=Joruri|Q1361891|WD=Q1361891}}'s ``{{ill|lt=Imoseyama Women's Teikun|Q11447158|WD=Q11447158}}'' {{Sfn|Seyama and Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}. === category|Category:Onamochi-jinja}} [[JA:大名持神社]] {{wikidata link|Q43594663}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Daimamochi Shrine |Image = [[File:Ōnamochi-jinja02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino Town|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}} Kawaraya 86[http://www .narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Daimamochi Shrine] (Nara Prefectural Shrine Agency). |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 23|Latitude seconds = 38.42 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 52|Longitude seconds = 8.33 |ISO = JP-29 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Daimyomochimitamokami|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime no Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Shonahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} |Divinity ={{ill|lt=Imoseyama|Q112620631|WD=Q112620631|1=Imoseyama}}|Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 17th|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}}{{Sfn|Daimamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}} |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Alias = Daigomiya |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Imoyama (Yoshino), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View of Imosayama from the opposite bank}}{{small|The torii gate of Daimamochi Shrine is on the center left. In the foreground is [[Yoshino River (Nara Prefecture)|Yoshino River]]. }}]] ``Dainamochi Shrine'' is located in Kawaraya, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino Town|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}}. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also commonly known as ``Daigo Shrine''.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}} is located near the junction of the former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ise Kaido|1=UNKNOWN}} and the former Higashi-Kumano Kaido, at the foot of the independent peak {{ill|lt=Mt. Imose (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|lt=Imoseyama|1=Imoseyama}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. . * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Daimyochimitamokami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime no Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}} * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Shonahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} In the literature, there is ``Daimomichi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', ``Dainamochi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'', and ``Engishiki'' extraordinary festival. It is seen as the ``Daimyochimitama Shrine Troupe'' in the Meishin Festival, and the enshrined deity has been one of the troupes of Onamuchi (another name for Okuninushi ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}})). It is not clear how Sukunabikona (Shona Hikona) was enshrined as a deity, but it was later named after a poem in volume 7 of "{{ill|lt=Manyoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}" (see below). There is a theory that it is a joint enshrinement of the gods, and another that it is the god of the mountain on the opposite bank. Furthermore, in the "Shinja Detailed Book", there are four deities enshrined: Daimyomochi-kami, Daimyochimitama-kami, the concubine Suserihime-kami, and Shunahikomakami. === History === === Outline === Founding date is unknown. {{ill|lt=Shisetsu (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|lt=Imoyamajuso|1=Imoseyama}} (249 meters above sea level[http://www.town.yoshino.nara.jp/kanko-event/meisho-kanko/shisetsu/ryumon-nakaryumon/imoyamajuso.html Imosayama is also called ``Imiyama,'' and it is said to be a sacred mountain because there is a taboo belief against cutting down trees to this day. k|Shintaiyama|Q1595289}} {{ill|lt=animism|Q43004|WD=Q43004|lt=primitive faith|1=Animism}} is presumed to be the founding shrine {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (gods)|2000}}.In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokukushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, {{ill|lt=Cheonan (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=In the year 3 of Tenan|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}'s ``Great God'' |Shinkai|Q11591025}} is recorded to the effect that he was promoted from Juichii to Shoichii. At that time, only {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}} in Yamato Province reached the highest rank of Shoichii, which was an extremely early promotion nationwide, but the reason for this is not necessarily clear {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1982}}{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. One theory is that it was highly revered because it was an important transportation hub connecting the imperial capital of {{ill|lt=Nara Basin|Q10942269|WD=Q10942269|1=Nara Basin}} to Yoshino {{Sfn|Isenyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) “{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, in Yamato Province {{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}, ``Daimamochi Shrine Meishin University As a "Monthly Aijo Niiname", it is listed at {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} and the {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=It is stipulated that at the Niinamesai|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944|1=Ainame Festival}} and {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, it is kept in a heihaku. Also, among the ``Engishiki'' special festivals, the ``Daimyo Mochimitama Shrine Troupe'' can be seen among the 285 Meishin Festivals in the Meishin Festival. In the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} period, ``Imose Shrine'' can be seen in ``Okami Bunshin Rui Shrine Tsunami Tsukio'' {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} period, ``Yamatoshi'' shows {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} Daikai-ji Temple (the location of the current shrine office) in the precincts, and a tidal pool in front of the shrine.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. It is said that the tide water gushes out from the Choobuchi, and purification was once performed there {{Sfn|Daimamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}.After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}In the first year of the Meiji era, Daikai-ji Temple was abolished, the original Buddha and Buddhist utensils were moved to Bukkoku-ji Temple, and the Daihanya Sutra was moved to Unsen-ji Temple.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from Juichii to Shoichii ('{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - Written as 'Omi Kikami'. === Precincts === File:Onamochi-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamochi-jinja, haiden.jpg|House of worship File:Onamochi-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office
{{small|Oldly located at Daikaiji Temple, {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. }} File:Onamochi-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === [[File:Onamochi-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shinuchisha}}]] There are the following four companies within the precincts . * Wakamiya Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Kotoshirosu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshirosu no Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. * Water Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Elephant Goddess|1=Mizuhanome}}. * Konpira Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}. * Inari Shrine - Enshrined deity: Inasoujin. === Cultural assets === === National Natural Monument === * Imoseyama Trees - For details, see "{{ill|lt=Imoseyama (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|1=Imoseyama}}". Designated on March 24, 1928 . === Appeared works === * From “{{ill|lt=Manyoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}” {{Cquote|Ohoanamichi, Sukunamikami's Shishi Shishi, Imose no Mt.|20px||"{{ill|lt=Man'yoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}" Volume 7 No. 1247"New Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 7 Man'yoshu (2)” Shogakukan, 2004 ({{ill|lt=Japan Knowledge|Q11309996|WD=Q11309996|1=JapanKnowledge}} edition), pp. 230-231. }} * From “{{ill|lt=Kokin Wakashu|Q1047167|WD=Q1047167|1=Kokin Wakashū}}” {{Cquote|Flowing and climbing in the mountains of Imose, Yoshiya world of the Yoshino river|20px||Unknown reader, "{{ill|lt=Kokin Wakashu|Q1047167|WD=Q1047167|1=Kokin Wakashū}}" {{Sfn|Seyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} It should be noted that the ``Mt. Imose'' that appears in these songs is not from here, but from Mt. Imose in Kii. (Wakayama Prefecture)||Seyama/Seyama}} ({{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ito District|Q1131309|WD=Q1131309|1=Ito District, Wakayama}}{{ill|lt=Katsuragi Town|Q1345594|WD=Q1345594|1=Katsuragi, Wakayama}}) There is also a theory that it is compared to {{Sfn|Seyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Another work set in Seyama and Seyama is {{ill|lt=Joruri|Q1361891|WD=Q1361891|1=Jōruri (music)}}'s ``{{ill|lt=Imoseyama Women's Teikun|Q11447158|WD=Q11447158|1=Imoseyama Onna Teikin}}'' {{Sfn|Seyama and Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Nakano Hano|authorlink=Nakano Hano|year=|chapter=Daimamochi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=National History Dictionary (Showa period)|Q11420603|WD=Q11420603|lt=National History Dictionary|1=Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Dainamochi Shrine (National History)}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial category|Category:Onamochi-jinja}} [[JA:大名持神社]] {{wikidata link|Q43594663}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Daimamochi Shrine |Image = [[File:Ōnamochi-jinja02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino Town|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}} Kawaraya 86[http://www .narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Daimamochi Shrine] (Nara Prefectural Shrine Agency). |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 23|Latitude seconds = 38.42 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 52|Longitude seconds = 8.33 |ISO = JP-29 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Daimyomochimitamokami|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime no Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Shonahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} |Divinity ={{ill|lt=Imoseyama|Q112620631|WD=Q112620631|1=Imoseyama}}|Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 17th|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}}{{Sfn|Daimamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}} |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Alias = Daigomiya |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Imoyama (Yoshino), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View of Imosayama from the opposite bank}}{{small|The torii gate of Daimamochi Shrine is on the center left. In the foreground is [[Yoshino River (Nara Prefecture)|Yoshino River]]. }}]] ``Dainamochi Shrine'' is located in Kawaraya, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino Town|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}}. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also commonly known as ``Daigo Shrine''.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}} is located near the junction of the former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ise Kaido|1=UNKNOWN}} and the former Higashi-Kumano Kaido, at the foot of the independent peak {{ill|lt=Mt. Imose (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|lt=Imoseyama|1=Imoseyama}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. . * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Daimyochimitamokami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime no Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}} * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Shonahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} In the literature, there is ``Daimomichi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', ``Dainamochi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'', and ``Engishiki'' extraordinary festival. It is seen as the ``Daimyochimitama Shrine Troupe'' in the Meishin Festival, and the enshrined deity has been one of the troupes of Onamuchi (another name for Okuninushi ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}})). It is not clear how Sukunabikona (Shona Hikona) was enshrined as a deity, but it was later named after a poem in volume 7 of "{{ill|lt=Manyoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}" (see below). There is a theory that it is a joint enshrinement of the gods, and another that it is the god of the mountain on the opposite bank. Furthermore, in the "Shinja Detailed Book", there are four deities enshrined: Daimyomochi-kami, Daimyochimitama-kami, the concubine Suserihime-kami, and Shunahikomakami. === History === === Outline === Founding date is unknown. {{ill|lt=Shisetsu (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|lt=Imoyamajuso|1=Imoseyama}} (249 meters above sea level[http://www.town.yoshino.nara.jp/kanko-event/meisho-kanko/shisetsu/ryumon-nakaryumon/imoyamajuso.html Imosayama is also called ``Imiyama,'' and it is said to be a sacred mountain because there is a taboo belief against cutting down trees to this day. k|Shintaiyama|Q1595289}} {{ill|lt=animism|Q43004|WD=Q43004|lt=primitive faith|1=Animism}} is presumed to be the founding shrine {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (gods)|2000}}.In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokukushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, {{ill|lt=Cheonan (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=In the year 3 of Tenan|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}'s ``Great God'' |Shinkai|Q11591025}} is recorded to the effect that he was promoted from Juichii to Shoichii. At that time, only {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}} in Yamato Province reached the highest rank of Shoichii, which was an extremely early promotion nationwide, but the reason for this is not necessarily clear {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1982}}{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. One theory is that it was highly revered because it was an important transportation hub connecting the imperial capital of {{ill|lt=Nara Basin|Q10942269|WD=Q10942269|1=Nara Basin}} to Yoshino {{Sfn|Isenyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) “{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, in Yamato Province {{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}, ``Daimamochi Shrine Meishin University As a "Monthly Aijo Niiname", it is listed at {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} and the {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=It is stipulated that at the Niinamesai|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944|1=Ainame Festival}} and {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, it is kept in a heihaku. Also, among the ``Engishiki'' special festivals, the ``Daimyo Mochimitama Shrine Troupe'' can be seen among the 285 Meishin Festivals in the Meishin Festival. In the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} period, ``Imose Shrine'' can be seen in ``Okami Bunshin Rui Shrine Tsunami Tsukio'' {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} period, ``Yamatoshi'' shows {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} Daikai-ji Temple (the location of the current shrine office) in the precincts, and a tidal pool in front of the shrine.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. It is said that the tide water gushes out from the Choobuchi, and purification was once performed there {{Sfn|Daimamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}.After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}In the first year of the Meiji era, Daikai-ji Temple was abolished, the original Buddha and Buddhist utensils were moved to Bukkoku-ji Temple, and the Daihanya Sutra was moved to Unsen-ji Temple.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from Juichii to Shoichii ('{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - Written as 'Omi Kikami'. === Precincts === File:Onamochi-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamochi-jinja, haiden.jpg|House of worship File:Onamochi-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office
{{small|Oldly located at Daikaiji Temple, {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. }} File:Onamochi-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === [[File:Onamochi-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shinuchisha}}]] There are the following four companies within the precincts . * Wakamiya Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Kotoshirosu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshirosu no Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. * Water Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Elephant Goddess|1=Mizuhanome}}. * Konpira Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}. * Inari Shrine - Enshrined deity: Inasoujin. === Cultural assets === === National Natural Monument === * Imoseyama Trees - For details, see "{{ill|lt=Imoseyama (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|1=Imoseyama}}". Designated on March 24, 1928 . === Appeared works === * From “{{ill|lt=Manyoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}” {{Cquote|Ohoanamichi, Sukunamikami's Shishi Shishi, Imose no Mt.|20px||"{{ill|lt=Man'yoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}" Volume 7 No. 1247"New Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 7 Man'yoshu (2)” Shogakukan, 2004 ({{ill|lt=Japan Knowledge|Q11309996|WD=Q11309996|1=JapanKnowledge}} edition), pp. 230-231. }} * From “{{ill|lt=Kokin Wakashu|Q1047167|WD=Q1047167|1=Kokin Wakashū}}” {{Cquote|Flowing and climbing in the mountains of Imose, Yoshiya world of the Yoshino river|20px||Unknown reader, "{{ill|lt=Kokin Wakashu|Q1047167|WD=Q1047167|1=Kokin Wakashū}}" {{Sfn|Seyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} It should be noted that the ``Mt. Imose'' that appears in these songs is not from here, but from Mt. Imose in Kii. (Wakayama Prefecture)||Seyama/Seyama}} ({{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ito District|Q1131309|WD=Q1131309|1=Ito District, Wakayama}}{{ill|lt=Katsuragi Town|Q1345594|WD=Q1345594|1=Katsuragi, Wakayama}}) There is also a theory that it is compared to {{Sfn|Seyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Another work set in Seyama and Seyama is {{ill|lt=Joruri|Q1361891|WD=Q1361891|1=Jōruri (music)}}'s ``{{ill|lt=Imoseyama Women's Teikun|Q11447158|WD=Q11447158|1=Imoseyama Onna Teikin}}'' {{Sfn|Seyama and Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Nakano Hano|authorlink=Nakano Hano|year=|chapter=Daimamochi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=National History Dictionary (Showa period)|Q11420603|WD=Q11420603|lt=National History Dictionary|1=Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Dainamochi Shrine (National History)}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino Town|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}} Kawaraya 86[http://www .narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Daimamochi Shrine] (Nara Prefectural Shrine Agency). |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 23|Latitude seconds = 38.42 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 52|Longitude seconds = 8.33 |ISO = JP-29 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Daimyomochimitamokami|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime no Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Shonahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} |Divinity ={{ill|lt=Imoseyama|Q112620631|WD=Q112620631|1=Imoseyama}}|Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 17th|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}}{{Sfn|Daimamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}} |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Alias = Daigomiya |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Imoyama (Yoshino), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View of Imosayama from the opposite bank}}{{small|The torii gate of Daimamochi Shrine is on the center left. In the foreground is [[Yoshino River (Nara Prefecture)|Yoshino River]]. }}]] ``Dainamochi Shrine'' is located in Kawaraya, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino Town|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}}. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also commonly known as ``Daigo Shrine''.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}} is located near the junction of the former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ise Kaido|1=UNKNOWN}} and the former Higashi-Kumano Kaido, at the foot of the independent peak {{ill|lt=Mt. Imose (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|lt=Imoseyama|1=Imoseyama}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. . * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Daimyochimitamokami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime no Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}} * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Shonahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} In the literature, there is ``Daimomichi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', ``Dainamochi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'', and ``Engishiki'' extraordinary festival. It is seen as the ``Daimyochimitama Shrine Troupe'' in the Meishin Festival, and the enshrined deity has been one of the troupes of Onamuchi (another name for Okuninushi ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}})). It is not clear how Sukunabikona (Shona Hikona) was enshrined as a deity, but it was later named after a poem in volume 7 of "{{ill|lt=Manyoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}" (see below). There is a theory that it is a joint enshrinement of the gods, and another that it is the god of the mountain on the opposite bank. Furthermore, in the "Shinja Detailed Book", there are four deities enshrined: Daimyomochi-kami, Daimyochimitama-kami, the concubine Suserihime-kami, and Shunahikomakami. === History === === Outline === Founding date is unknown. {{ill|lt=Shisetsu (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|lt=Imoyamajuso|1=Imoseyama}} (249 meters above sea level[http://www.town.yoshino.nara.jp/kanko-event/meisho-kanko/shisetsu/ryumon-nakaryumon/imoyamajuso.html Imosayama is also called ``Imiyama,'' and it is said to be a sacred mountain because there is a taboo belief against cutting down trees to this day. k|Shintaiyama|Q1595289}} {{ill|lt=animism|Q43004|WD=Q43004|lt=primitive faith|1=Animism}} is presumed to be the founding shrine {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (gods)|2000}}.In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokukushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, {{ill|lt=Cheonan (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=In the year 3 of Tenan|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}'s ``Great God'' |Shinkai|Q11591025}} is recorded to the effect that he was promoted from Juichii to Shoichii. At that time, only {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}} in Yamato Province reached the highest rank of Shoichii, which was an extremely early promotion nationwide, but the reason for this is not necessarily clear {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1982}}{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. One theory is that it was highly revered because it was an important transportation hub connecting the imperial capital of {{ill|lt=Nara Basin|Q10942269|WD=Q10942269|1=Nara Basin}} to Yoshino {{Sfn|Isenyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) “{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, in Yamato Province {{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}, ``Daimamochi Shrine Meishin University As a "Monthly Aijo Niiname", it is listed at {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} and the {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=It is stipulated that at the Niinamesai|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944|1=Ainame Festival}} and {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, it is kept in a heihaku. Also, among the ``Engishiki'' special festivals, the ``Daimyo Mochimitama Shrine Troupe'' can be seen among the 285 Meishin Festivals in the Meishin Festival. In the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} period, ``Imose Shrine'' can be seen in ``Okami Bunshin Rui Shrine Tsunami Tsukio'' {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} period, ``Yamatoshi'' shows {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} Daikai-ji Temple (the location of the current shrine office) in the precincts, and a tidal pool in front of the shrine.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. It is said that the tide water gushes out from the Choobuchi, and purification was once performed there {{Sfn|Daimamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}.After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}In the first year of the Meiji era, Daikai-ji Temple was abolished, the original Buddha and Buddhist utensils were moved to Bukkoku-ji Temple, and the Daihanya Sutra was moved to Unsen-ji Temple.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from Juichii to Shoichii ('{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - Written as 'Omi Kikami'. === Precincts === File:Onamochi-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamochi-jinja, haiden.jpg|House of worship File:Onamochi-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office
{{small|Oldly located at Daikaiji Temple, {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. }} File:Onamochi-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === [[File:Onamochi-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shinuchisha}}]] There are the following four companies within the precincts . * Wakamiya Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Kotoshirosu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshirosu no Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. * Water Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Elephant Goddess|1=Mizuhanome}}. * Konpira Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}. * Inari Shrine - Enshrined deity: Inasoujin. === Cultural assets === === National Natural Monument === * Imoseyama Trees - For details, see "{{ill|lt=Imoseyama (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|1=Imoseyama}}". Designated on March 24, 1928 . === Appeared works === * From “{{ill|lt=Manyoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}” {{Cquote|Ohoanamichi, Sukunamikami's Shishi Shishi, Imose no Mt.|20px||"{{ill|lt=Man'yoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}" Volume 7 No. 1247"New Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 7 Man'yoshu (2)” Shogakukan, 2004 ({{ill|lt=Japan Knowledge|Q11309996|WD=Q11309996|1=JapanKnowledge}} edition), pp. 230-231. }} * From “{{ill|lt=Kokin Wakashu|Q1047167|WD=Q1047167|1=Kokin Wakashū}}” {{Cquote|Flowing and climbing in the mountains of Imose, Yoshiya world of the Yoshino river|20px||Unknown reader, "{{ill|lt=Kokin Wakashu|Q1047167|WD=Q1047167|1=Kokin Wakashū}}" {{Sfn|Seyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} It should be noted that the ``Mt. Imose'' that appears in these songs is not from here, but from Mt. Imose in Kii. (Wakayama Prefecture)||Seyama/Seyama}} ({{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ito District|Q1131309|WD=Q1131309|1=Ito District, Wakayama}}{{ill|lt=Katsuragi Town|Q1345594|WD=Q1345594|1=Katsuragi, Wakayama}}) There is also a theory that it is compared to {{Sfn|Seyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Another work set in Seyama and Seyama is {{ill|lt=Joruri|Q1361891|WD=Q1361891|1=Jōruri (music)}}'s ``{{ill|lt=Imoseyama Women's Teikun|Q11447158|WD=Q11447158|1=Imoseyama Onna Teikin}}'' {{Sfn|Seyama and Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Nakano Hano|authorlink=Nakano Hano|year=|chapter=Daimamochi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=National History Dictionary (Showa period)|Q11420603|WD=Q11420603|lt=National History Dictionary|1=Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa category|Category:Onamochi-jinja}} [[JA:大名持神社]] {{wikidata link|Q43594663}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Daimamochi Shrine |Image = [[File:Ōnamochi-jinja02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino Town|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}} Kawaraya 86[http://www .narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Daimamochi Shrine] (Nara Prefectural Shrine Agency). |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 23|Latitude seconds = 38.42 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 52|Longitude seconds = 8.33 |ISO = JP-29 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Daimyomochimitamokami|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime no Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Shonahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} |Divinity ={{ill|lt=Imoseyama|Q112620631|WD=Q112620631|1=Imoseyama}}|Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 17th|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}}{{Sfn|Daimamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}} |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Alias = Daigomiya |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Imoyama (Yoshino), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View of Imosayama from the opposite bank}}{{small|The torii gate of Daimamochi Shrine is on the center left. In the foreground is [[Yoshino River (Nara Prefecture)|Yoshino River]]. }}]] ``Dainamochi Shrine'' is located in Kawaraya, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino Town|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}}. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also commonly known as ``Daigo Shrine''.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}} is located near the junction of the former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ise Kaido|1=UNKNOWN}} and the former Higashi-Kumano Kaido, at the foot of the independent peak {{ill|lt=Mt. Imose (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|lt=Imoseyama|1=Imoseyama}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. . * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Daimyochimitamokami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime no Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}} * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Shonahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} In the literature, there is ``Daimomichi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', ``Dainamochi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'', and ``Engishiki'' extraordinary festival. It is seen as the ``Daimyochimitama Shrine Troupe'' in the Meishin Festival, and the enshrined deity has been one of the troupes of Onamuchi (another name for Okuninushi ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}})). It is not clear how Sukunabikona (Shona Hikona) was enshrined as a deity, but it was later named after a poem in volume 7 of "{{ill|lt=Manyoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}" (see below). There is a theory that it is a joint enshrinement of the gods, and another that it is the god of the mountain on the opposite bank. Furthermore, in the "Shinja Detailed Book", there are four deities enshrined: Daimyomochi-kami, Daimyochimitama-kami, the concubine Suserihime-kami, and Shunahikomakami. === History === === Outline === Founding date is unknown. {{ill|lt=Shisetsu (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|lt=Imoyamajuso|1=Imoseyama}} (249 meters above sea level[http://www.town.yoshino.nara.jp/kanko-event/meisho-kanko/shisetsu/ryumon-nakaryumon/imoyamajuso.html Imosayama is also called ``Imiyama,'' and it is said to be a sacred mountain because there is a taboo belief against cutting down trees to this day. k|Shintaiyama|Q1595289}} {{ill|lt=animism|Q43004|WD=Q43004|lt=primitive faith|1=Animism}} is presumed to be the founding shrine {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (gods)|2000}}.In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokukushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, {{ill|lt=Cheonan (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=In the year 3 of Tenan|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}'s ``Great God'' |Shinkai|Q11591025}} is recorded to the effect that he was promoted from Juichii to Shoichii. At that time, only {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}} in Yamato Province reached the highest rank of Shoichii, which was an extremely early promotion nationwide, but the reason for this is not necessarily clear {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1982}}{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. One theory is that it was highly revered because it was an important transportation hub connecting the imperial capital of {{ill|lt=Nara Basin|Q10942269|WD=Q10942269|1=Nara Basin}} to Yoshino {{Sfn|Isenyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) “{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, in Yamato Province {{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}, ``Daimamochi Shrine Meishin University As a "Monthly Aijo Niiname", it is listed at {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} and the {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=It is stipulated that at the Niinamesai|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944|1=Ainame Festival}} and {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, it is kept in a heihaku. Also, among the ``Engishiki'' special festivals, the ``Daimyo Mochimitama Shrine Troupe'' can be seen among the 285 Meishin Festivals in the Meishin Festival. In the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} period, ``Imose Shrine'' can be seen in ``Okami Bunshin Rui Shrine Tsunami Tsukio'' {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} period, ``Yamatoshi'' shows {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} Daikai-ji Temple (the location of the current shrine office) in the precincts, and a tidal pool in front of the shrine.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. It is said that the tide water gushes out from the Choobuchi, and purification was once performed there {{Sfn|Daimamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}.After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}In the first year of the Meiji era, Daikai-ji Temple was abolished, the original Buddha and Buddhist utensils were moved to Bukkoku-ji Temple, and the Daihanya Sutra was moved to Unsen-ji Temple.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from Juichii to Shoichii ('{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - Written as 'Omi Kikami'. === Precincts === File:Onamochi-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamochi-jinja, haiden.jpg|House of worship File:Onamochi-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office
{{small|Oldly located at Daikaiji Temple, {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. }} File:Onamochi-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === [[File:Onamochi-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shinuchisha}}]] There are the following four companies within the precincts . * Wakamiya Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Kotoshirosu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshirosu no Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. * Water Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Elephant Goddess|1=Mizuhanome}}. * Konpira Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}. * Inari Shrine - Enshrined deity: Inasoujin. === Cultural assets === === National Natural Monument === * Imoseyama Trees - For details, see "{{ill|lt=Imoseyama (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|1=Imoseyama}}". Designated on March 24, 1928 . === Appeared works === * From “{{ill|lt=Manyoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}” {{Cquote|Ohoanamichi, Sukunamikami's Shishi Shishi, Imose no Mt.|20px||"{{ill|lt=Man'yoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}" Volume 7 No. 1247"New Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 7 Man'yoshu (2)” Shogakukan, 2004 ({{ill|lt=Japan Knowledge|Q11309996|WD=Q11309996|1=JapanKnowledge}} edition), pp. 230-231. }} * From “{{ill|lt=Kokin Wakashu|Q1047167|WD=Q1047167|1=Kokin Wakashū}}” {{Cquote|Flowing and climbing in the mountains of Imose, Yoshiya world of the Yoshino river|20px||Unknown reader, "{{ill|lt=Kokin Wakashu|Q1047167|WD=Q1047167|1=Kokin Wakashū}}" {{Sfn|Seyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} It should be noted that the ``Mt. Imose'' that appears in these songs is not from here, but from Mt. Imose in Kii. (Wakayama Prefecture)||Seyama/Seyama}} ({{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ito District|Q1131309|WD=Q1131309|1=Ito District, Wakayama}}{{ill|lt=Katsuragi Town|Q1345594|WD=Q1345594|1=Katsuragi, Wakayama}}) There is also a theory that it is compared to {{Sfn|Seyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Another work set in Seyama and Seyama is {{ill|lt=Joruri|Q1361891|WD=Q1361891|1=Jōruri (music)}}'s ``{{ill|lt=Imoseyama Women's Teikun|Q11447158|WD=Q11447158|1=Imoseyama Onna Teikin}}'' {{Sfn|Seyama and Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Nakano Hano|authorlink=Nakano Hano|year=|chapter=Daimamochi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=National History Dictionary (Showa period)|Q11420603|WD=Q11420603|lt=National History Dictionary|1=Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Dainamochi Shrine (National History)}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Daimamochi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/699}} "Meiji Shrine Journal Materials Fuken Gosha Volume 1"] (National Diet Library Digital Collection) Reference frames 699-700. * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=|title={{ill|lt=Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical category|Category:Onamochi-jinja}} [[JA:大名持神社]] {{wikidata link|Q43594663}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Daimamochi Shrine |Image = [[File:Ōnamochi-jinja02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino Town|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}} Kawaraya 86[http://www .narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%A2%E8%A1%8C/%E5%A4%A7%E5%90%8D%E6%8C%81%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Daimamochi Shrine] (Nara Prefectural Shrine Agency). |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 23|Latitude seconds = 38.42 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 52|Longitude seconds = 8.33 |ISO = JP-29 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Daimyomochimitamokami|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime no Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Shonahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} |Divinity ={{ill|lt=Imoseyama|Q112620631|WD=Q112620631|1=Imoseyama}}|Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 17th|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}}{{Sfn|Daimamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}} |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Alias = Daigomiya |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Imoyama (Yoshino), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View of Imosayama from the opposite bank}}{{small|The torii gate of Daimamochi Shrine is on the center left. In the foreground is [[Yoshino River (Nara Prefecture)|Yoshino River]]. }}]] ``Dainamochi Shrine'' is located in Kawaraya, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino Town|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}}. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also commonly known as ``Daigo Shrine''.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}} is located near the junction of the former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ise Kaido|1=UNKNOWN}} and the former Higashi-Kumano Kaido, at the foot of the independent peak {{ill|lt=Mt. Imose (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|lt=Imoseyama|1=Imoseyama}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. . * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Daimyochimitamokami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime no Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}} * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Shonahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} In the literature, there is ``Daimomichi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', ``Dainamochi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'', and ``Engishiki'' extraordinary festival. It is seen as the ``Daimyochimitama Shrine Troupe'' in the Meishin Festival, and the enshrined deity has been one of the troupes of Onamuchi (another name for Okuninushi ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}})). It is not clear how Sukunabikona (Shona Hikona) was enshrined as a deity, but it was later named after a poem in volume 7 of "{{ill|lt=Manyoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}" (see below). There is a theory that it is a joint enshrinement of the gods, and another that it is the god of the mountain on the opposite bank. Furthermore, in the "Shinja Detailed Book", there are four deities enshrined: Daimyomochi-kami, Daimyochimitama-kami, the concubine Suserihime-kami, and Shunahikomakami. === History === === Outline === Founding date is unknown. {{ill|lt=Shisetsu (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|lt=Imoyamajuso|1=Imoseyama}} (249 meters above sea level[http://www.town.yoshino.nara.jp/kanko-event/meisho-kanko/shisetsu/ryumon-nakaryumon/imoyamajuso.html Imosayama is also called ``Imiyama,'' and it is said to be a sacred mountain because there is a taboo belief against cutting down trees to this day. k|Shintaiyama|Q1595289}} {{ill|lt=animism|Q43004|WD=Q43004|lt=primitive faith|1=Animism}} is presumed to be the founding shrine {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (gods)|2000}}.In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokukushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, {{ill|lt=Cheonan (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=In the year 3 of Tenan|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}'s ``Great God'' |Shinkai|Q11591025}} is recorded to the effect that he was promoted from Juichii to Shoichii. At that time, only {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}} in Yamato Province reached the highest rank of Shoichii, which was an extremely early promotion nationwide, but the reason for this is not necessarily clear {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1982}}{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. One theory is that it was highly revered because it was an important transportation hub connecting the imperial capital of {{ill|lt=Nara Basin|Q10942269|WD=Q10942269|1=Nara Basin}} to Yoshino {{Sfn|Isenyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) “{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, in Yamato Province {{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}, ``Daimamochi Shrine Meishin University As a "Monthly Aijo Niiname", it is listed at {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} and the {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=It is stipulated that at the Niinamesai|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944|1=Ainame Festival}} and {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, it is kept in a heihaku. Also, among the ``Engishiki'' special festivals, the ``Daimyo Mochimitama Shrine Troupe'' can be seen among the 285 Meishin Festivals in the Meishin Festival. In the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} period, ``Imose Shrine'' can be seen in ``Okami Bunshin Rui Shrine Tsunami Tsukio'' {{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} period, ``Yamatoshi'' shows {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} Daikai-ji Temple (the location of the current shrine office) in the precincts, and a tidal pool in front of the shrine.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. It is said that the tide water gushes out from the Choobuchi, and purification was once performed there {{Sfn|Daimamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}.After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}In the first year of the Meiji era, Daikai-ji Temple was abolished, the original Buddha and Buddhist utensils were moved to Bukkoku-ji Temple, and the Daihanya Sutra was moved to Unsen-ji Temple.{{Sfn|Dainamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from Juichii to Shoichii ('{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - Written as 'Omi Kikami'. === Precincts === File:Onamochi-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamochi-jinja, haiden.jpg|House of worship File:Onamochi-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office
{{small|Oldly located at Daikaiji Temple, {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. }} File:Onamochi-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === [[File:Onamochi-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shinuchisha}}]] There are the following four companies within the precincts . * Wakamiya Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Kotoshirosu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshirosu no Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. * Water Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Elephant Goddess|1=Mizuhanome}}. * Konpira Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}. * Inari Shrine - Enshrined deity: Inasoujin. === Cultural assets === === National Natural Monument === * Imoseyama Trees - For details, see "{{ill|lt=Imoseyama (Nara Prefecture)|Q112339073|WD=Q112339073|1=Imoseyama}}". Designated on March 24, 1928 . === Appeared works === * From “{{ill|lt=Manyoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}” {{Cquote|Ohoanamichi, Sukunamikami's Shishi Shishi, Imose no Mt.|20px||"{{ill|lt=Man'yoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}" Volume 7 No. 1247"New Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 7 Man'yoshu (2)” Shogakukan, 2004 ({{ill|lt=Japan Knowledge|Q11309996|WD=Q11309996|1=JapanKnowledge}} edition), pp. 230-231. }} * From “{{ill|lt=Kokin Wakashu|Q1047167|WD=Q1047167|1=Kokin Wakashū}}” {{Cquote|Flowing and climbing in the mountains of Imose, Yoshiya world of the Yoshino river|20px||Unknown reader, "{{ill|lt=Kokin Wakashu|Q1047167|WD=Q1047167|1=Kokin Wakashū}}" {{Sfn|Seyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} It should be noted that the ``Mt. Imose'' that appears in these songs is not from here, but from Mt. Imose in Kii. (Wakayama Prefecture)||Seyama/Seyama}} ({{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ito District|Q1131309|WD=Q1131309|1=Ito District, Wakayama}}{{ill|lt=Katsuragi Town|Q1345594|WD=Q1345594|1=Katsuragi, Wakayama}}) There is also a theory that it is compared to {{Sfn|Seyama/Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Another work set in Seyama and Seyama is {{ill|lt=Joruri|Q1361891|WD=Q1361891|1=Jōruri (music)}}'s ``{{ill|lt=Imoseyama Women's Teikun|Q11447158|WD=Q11447158|1=Imoseyama Onna Teikin}}'' {{Sfn|Seyama and Seyama (Heibonsha)|1981}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Nakano Hano|authorlink=Nakano Hano|year=|chapter=Daimamochi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=National History Dictionary (Showa period)|Q11420603|WD=Q11420603|lt=National History Dictionary|1=Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Dainamochi Shrine (National History)}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Editorial Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/699}} "Meiji Shrine Journal Materials Fuken Gosha Volume 1"] (National Diet Library Digital
